data.title: Stripe in talks with banks about stablecoins - report
data.description:
Payment tech company Stripe (STRIP) has held early talks with banks regarding the potential use of stablecoins, one more indication that digital assets' role in global payments is gaining traction, according to a media report on Friday.

"This is not something that banks are just kind of brushing away or treating as a fad," Stripe co-founder and President John Collison told Bloomberg News. "Banks are very interested in how they should be integrated with stablecoins into their product offerings as well."

Stablecoins are designed to hold a constant value, often against a fiat currency, such as the U.S. dollar.

The company has recently introduced a range of products related to stablecoins, including a platform that allows fintechs to start their own stablecoin-linked card programs for customers.

Other payment tech companies have also ventured into the stablecoin space. In 2023, PayPayl (PYPL) launched its own stablecoin, PayPal USD (PY-USD).

Stripe (STRIP) recently closed its $1.1B acquisition of stablecoin platform Bridge.

“A lot of our future payment volume is going to be in stablecoins,” Collison told Bloomberg. “This is, for sure, a big part of our business on a go-forward basis.”

data.title: Invitation Homes estimates slowdown in renewal lease growth but higher new lease growth
data.description:
Single-family residential REIT Invitation Homes (NYSE:INVH) announced in an investor presentation on Friday an expected slowdown in the quarter-to-date renewal lease rate growth, but an increase in new lease rate growth.

Shares were +0.57% pre-market to $33.69.

As part of the company's preliminary April-May quarter-to-date same-store results, INVH delivered renewal lease rate growth of 4.7% vs. 5.2% in Q1. New lease rate growth is estimated at 2.5% vs. a decline of 0.1% in Q1.

Blended lease rate growth is expected at 4.1%, compared to 3.6% in the prior quarter.

Average occupancy is estimated at 97.3%, up from 97.2% in the previous quarter.

data.title: Zymeworks gets China's conditional approval for biliary tract cancer treatment
data.description:
• Zymeworks (NASDAQ:ZYME) said on Friday that the China health regulator has given conditional approval to zanidatamab for the treatment of metastatic HER2-positive biliary tract cancer.
  • The approval was secured by Zymeworks’ Asia Pacific partner, BeOne Medicines, under their existing license and collaboration agreement.
  • So far, Zymeworks has received $61M in upfront and milestone payments from BeOne, along with co-development funding to support zanidatamab’s clinical trials.

data.title: Canopy Growth falls after Q4 earnings miss
data.description:
Shares of Canopy Growth (NASDAQ:CGC) lost ~12% in the premarket on Friday after the Canadian cannabis player reported its Q4 results for fiscal 2025, falling well short of Street forecasts as its international operations and Storz & Bickel segment  underperformed,

The Ontario-based company reported C$65.0M in net revenue for the quarter, missing the consensus by $5.5M with ~11% YoY growth, even as Canopy’s (NASDAQ:CGC) Canadian segment offset a sharp decline in international and Storz & Bickel sales.

While Canada cannabis sales improved ~4% YoY to C$40M, backed by ~13% growth in medical cannabis, CGC’s international cannabis segment contracted ~35% YoY to generate C$8M in net revenue, and the company’s Storz & Bickel brand added C$17M, implying ~23% drop.

CGC’s adjusted gross margin fell 200 bps from the prior year period to ~19%, driving its adjusted EBITDA and GAAP loss per share to C$9M and C$1.43. Both figures missed Wall Street forecasts of $3.9M and $0.49, according to Bloomberg data, despite ~39% YoY and ~35% YoY improvement, respectively.

While the company’s free cash outflow worsened by ~60% YoY to C$36M during Q4, its full-year free cash outflow fell ~24% YoY to C$177M, mainly due to lower interest payments as the company’s total debt reached C$304M as of Mar. 31 compared to C$597M a year ago.

 “We are committed to achieving positive adjusted EBITDA in the near term and positive free cash flow over time as we accelerate growth across our global medical cannabis businesses and improve margins,” CEO Luc Mongeau remarked.

The company announced plans to improve gross margin and adj. EBITDA, driven by annualized cost savings of at least C$20M over the next 12–18 months from cost reduction opportunities identified in SG&A and Cost of Sales during Q4.

data.title: Trump says China has violated trade agreement with U.S.
data.description:
President Donald Trump accused China of violating a trade agreement with the U.S.

Trump's statement comes after Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told Fox News that the U.S. trade talks with China are "a bit stalled."

"China, perhaps not surprisingly to some, HAS TOTALLY VIOLATED ITS AGREEMENT WITH US. So much for being Mr. NICE GUY!," Trump posted on the Truth Social platform. 

The U.S. and China reached a temporary truce earlier this month, with both countries agreeing to roll back tariff hikes till mid-August. Even so, the U.S. has moved ahead with tech restrictions on China, while Beijing has yet to significantly ease curbs on critical mineral exports.

"The very high Tariffs I set made it virtually impossible for China to TRADE into the United States marketplace which is, by far, number one in the World. We went, in effect, COLD TURKEY with China, and it was devastating for them," Trump said in the post. 

Bessent had earlier said the countries' presidents, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping will need to speak to each other to reach a deal.

"I made a FAST DEAL with China in order to save them from what I thought was going to be a very bad situation, and I didn’t want to see that happen. Because of this deal, everything quickly stabilized and China got back to business as usual," Trump said, adding later in the post that China had violated the deal.

data.title: Unity Software in focus on back of Jefferies upgrade
data.description:
• Unity Software (NYSE:U) was in focus on Friday as Jefferies upgraded the software company, citing several reasons, including an improved Vector ad model.
  • “We are upgrading U based on the view the improved Vector ad model can drive accelerating rev growth in FY26 and beyond,” analyst Brent Thill wrote in a note to clients. “While early, the green shoots we're seeing from Vector, upcoming feature upgrades, and new [management] give us confidence in at least HSD% rev growth in FY26. With high incremental EBITDA margins in the Grow business, we believe the risk-reward is favorable as see potential for significant EBITDA upside.” 
  • Thill raised his rating on Unity to Buy from Hold and upped his price target to $29 from $22.
  • Aside from the growth acceleration tied to Vector, Thill said Unity is seeing stability in its Create segment, which should set the path for double-digit percentage growth; a renewed focus on cost discipline, which should boost margins to 50% or more; a renewed confidence in the company's management, with 100% of the C-Suite having turned over since the ironSource acquisition 2.5 years ago; and a “reasonable” valuation, given the growth potential.

data.title: Sign of the times? This stock is up 2700% this week after initiating an Ethereum buying strategy
data.description:
SharpLink Gaming (NASDAQ:SBET) continues to soar after the company announced a major strategic shift with a $425 million private placement to fund an Ethereum-focused treasury strategy.

The company entered into securities purchase agreements for a private investment in public equity for the purchase and sale of ~69.1M shares. SharpLink (NASDAQ:SBET) said it intends to use the funds to acquire the native cryptocurrency of the Ethereum blockchain commonly referred to as ETH pending identification of working capital needs and other general corporate purposes.

Minneapolis-based SharpLink Gaming (SBET) was previously known as an online performance marketing company specializing in affiliate marketing for the sports betting and online casino gaming industries before its apparent pivot. The company's mission was to connect sports fans and digital platforms with licensed sportsbook and casino partners through targeted, data-driven marketing strategies that focus on user acquisition, retention, and conversion.

Shares of SharpLink Gaming (SBET) rose 22.5% in premarket trading on Friday, following a 172% pop on Thursday. The stock is up more than 2700% from where it stood a week ago.

Ethereum (ETH-USD) is up 4.4% in the last week. Some Ethereum ETFs include 2x Ether ETF(BATS:ETHU), CoinShares Bitcoin and Ether Strategy ETF (NASDAQ:BTF), ProShares Ether ETF (NYSEARCA:EETH), iShares Ethereum Trust ETF (NASDAQ:ETHA), VanEck Ethereum ETF (BATS:ETHV), Fidelity Ethereum Fund ETF (BATS:FETH), and Roundhill Ether Covered Call Strategy ETF (BATS:YETH).

data.title: NetApp provides conservative guidance, analysts note improving margins
data.description:
NetApp (NASDAQ:NTAP) shares dipped after the enterprise storage and data management company provided tighter revenue guidance than the market expected in its latest earnings, but analysts noted margins for fiscal 2026 were higher than anticipated.

"F26 revenue guidance was $6.75bn at the mid-point, which is below Street expectations of $6.84bn," said Bank of America Securities analysts, led by Wamsi Mohan, in an investor note. "Gross margin & operating margin guidance of 71.5% & 29.3% at the mid-point came in above Street expectations of 70.9% & 28.3%. The margins are impacted early in F26 by longer than expected weakness in product GMs but offset by Public Cloud GMs at the high-end of the 75-80% l-t range as well as better mix."

The previously announced divestiture of Spot to Flexera for about $100M will also improve NetApp's gross margins further in its Public Cloud segment, Mohan noted.

BofA reiterated its Neutral rating and nudged its price target to $121 from $120.

"We suspect there is a dose of conservatism in the guide as well given this is the 1st guide from the new CFO (Wissam Jabre)," according to Evercore ISI analysts, led by Amit Daryanani, in a note. "From a product perspective, All Flash array continues to drive Hybrid Cloud, growing 14% to reach an ARR of $4.1B, representing ~66% of the Hybrid Cloud segment."

"While NTAP faces a more dynamic macro-environment, we think the company is being conservative with their guide and that there could be upside from enterprise AI deployments, stability on government spending, and a better margin/commodity environment," Evercore said. "Specifically, NTAP noted sizable AI deals with large enterprises that are expected to close later this year."

NetApp indicated that artificial intelligence inferencing presents a greater opportunity than model training.

"We are helping customers deploy AI inferencing in production today and expect fiscal year 2026 to be a pivotal year for enterprise AI storage, with the opportunity outstripping that of model training," said NetApp CEO George Kurian, during Thursday's earnings call.

Flash competitor Pure Storage (PSTG) was relatively flat during early Friday trading. Dell Technologies (DELL) and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) had both slipped 1%.

data.title: STMicroelectronics declares $0.09 dividend
data.description:
• STMicroelectronics (NYSE:STM) declares $0.09/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.

  • Payable March. 31; for shareholders of record March. 24; ex-div March. 24.

data.title: Lowe's raises dividend by 4.3% to $1.20
data.description:
• Lowe's (NYSE:LOW) declares $1.20/share quarterly dividend, 4.3% increase from prior dividend of $1.15.
  • Forward yield 2.13%
  • Payable Aug. 6; for shareholders of record July 23; ex-div July 23.
  • See LOW Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
